data.content:
### Protection Bypass Request with Header

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/deployment-protection/methods-to-bypass-deployment-protection/protection-bypass-automation

Bypass Vercel Deployment Protection by including the x-vercel-protection-bypass header with the generated secret. This is the recommended method for automated tooling to bypass protection mechanisms on all project deployments.

```APIDOC
## HTTP Request with Protection Bypass Header

### Description
Bypass Vercel Deployment Protection by including the required header with the generated secret value.

### Headers
- **x-vercel-protection-bypass** (string) - Required - The generated secret for the project to bypass protection
- **x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie** (boolean|string) - Optional - Set to `true` or `samesitenone` to set authorization bypass as a cookie for follow-up requests

### Request Example
```
GET /deployment-url HTTP/1.1
Host: your-deployment.vercel.app
x-vercel-protection-bypass: your-generated-secret
x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie: true
```

### Query Parameter Alternative
If headers cannot be specified, use query parameters instead:
```
GET /deployment-url?x-vercel-protection-bypass=your-generated-secret&x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie=true
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
The deployment is accessed without requiring authentication.

### Notes
- Using a header is strongly recommended over query parameters
- The secret is automatically added as `VERCEL_AUTOMATION_BYPASS_SECRET` environment variable during deployment
- Regenerating the secret will invalidate previous deployments
- For iframe access, set `x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie` to `samesitenone` to set `SameSite=None`
```

--------------------------------

### Playwright Configuration with Protection Bypass

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/deployment-protection/methods-to-bypass-deployment-protection/protection-bypass-automation

Configure Playwright to use Protection Bypass for Automation by setting the x-vercel-protection-bypass header in extraHTTPHeaders. This enables E2E tests to bypass deployment protection automatically.

```APIDOC
## Playwright E2E Testing Configuration

### Description
Configure Playwright test runner to automatically bypass Vercel Deployment Protection using the generated automation secret.

### Implementation

#### File: playwright.config.ts
```typescript
import { PlaywrightTestConfig } from '@playwright/test';

const config: PlaywrightTestConfig = {
  use: {
    extraHTTPHeaders: {
      'x-vercel-protection-bypass': process.env.VERCEL_AUTOMATION_BYPASS_SECRET,
      'x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie': true // or 'samesitenone' for iframe access
    }
  }
};

export default config;
```

### Configuration Details
- **extraHTTPHeaders** - Object containing HTTP headers sent with every request
- **x-vercel-protection-bypass** - Required header with value from `VERCEL_AUTOMATION_BYPASS_SECRET` environment variable
- **x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie** - Optional header to persist bypass across follow-up requests
  - Set to `true` for standard same-site requests
  - Set to `samesitenone` for cross-origin requests or iframe scenarios

### Environment Variable
The secret is automatically injected as `VERCEL_AUTOMATION_BYPASS_SECRET` system environment variable during deployment.

### Notes
- Regenerating the secret requires redeploying your app to use the new value
- This configuration applies to all HTTP requests made by Playwright tests
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Playwright to Bypass Vercel Deployment Protection

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/deployment-protection/methods-to-bypass-deployment-protection/protection-bypass-automation

This Playwright configuration snippet demonstrates how to integrate the Vercel Protection Bypass feature into end-to-end tests. It utilizes `extraHTTPHeaders` to set the bypass secret from an environment variable and optionally enables the bypass cookie for subsequent requests.

```TypeScript
const config: PlaywrightTestConfig = {
  use: {
    extraHTTPHeaders: {
      'x-vercel-protection-bypass': process.env.VERCEL_AUTOMATION_BYPASS_SECRET,
      'x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie': true | 'samesitenone' (optional)
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Set HTTP Headers for Vercel Protection Bypass

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/deployment-protection/methods-to-bypass-deployment-protection/protection-bypass-automation

Illustrates the required and optional HTTP headers for bypassing Vercel Deployment Protection. The `x-vercel-protection-bypass` header uses a generated secret, while `x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie` can set a bypass cookie, with an option for `samesitenone` for cross-site contexts.

```HTTP
x-vercel-protection-bypass: your-generated-secret (required)
x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie: true (optional)
x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie: samesitenone (optional)
```

--------------------------------

### Website Deployment Schema - Protection Bypass

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/reference/endpoints/projects/find-a-project-by-id-or-name

Manages protection bypass rules for deployments, supporting both integration automation bypasses and general automation bypasses with creation metadata.

```APIDOC
## Deployment Protection Bypass

### Description
Defines protection bypass configurations that allow deployments to bypass certain protections. Supports multiple bypass scopes with creation tracking and optional integration details.

### Schema
```
protectionBypass:
  type: object
  additionalProperties: true
```

### Bypass Types

#### Integration Automation Bypass
- **createdAt** (number) - Required - Unix timestamp of bypass creation
- **createdBy** (string) - Required - User or system that created the bypass
- **scope** (string) - Required - Enum: `integration-automation-bypass`
- **integrationId** (string) - Required - ID of the integration for which bypass applies
- **configurationId** (string) - Required - Configuration ID associated with the bypass

#### Automation Bypass
- **createdAt** (number) - Required - Unix timestamp of bypass creation
- **createdBy** (string) - Required - User or system that created the bypass
- **scope** (string) - Required - Enum: `automation-bypass`

### Request Example
```json
{
  "bypass_1": {
    "createdAt": 1672531200,
    "createdBy": "user_123",
    "scope": "integration-automation-bypass",
    "integrationId": "int_abc123",
    "configurationId": "cfg_def456"
  },
  "bypass_2": {
    "createdAt": 1672531300,
    "createdBy": "system",
    "scope": "automation-bypass"
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Enable or Update Vercel Authentication via API

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/deployment-protection/methods-to-protect-deployments/vercel-authentication

API request body to enable or update Vercel Authentication on an existing project using the Vercel API endpoint. Supports three deployment type options: prod_deployment_urls_and_all_previews (standard protection), all (all deployments), or preview (preview deployments only). Returns the updated project configuration.

```json
{
  "ssoProtection": {
    "deploymentType": "prod_deployment_urls_and_all_previews" | "all" | "preview"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Default Deployment Protection Settings

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/reference/endpoints/teams/update-a-team

Configure default deployment protection policies for new projects. Includes password protection and SSO protection settings that apply to project deployments.

```APIDOC
## Default Deployment Protection Configuration

### Description
Set default deployment protection settings for new projects.

### Properties

#### defaultDeploymentProtection

##### passwordProtection
- **deploymentType** (string) - Required - Specify if password protection applies to all deployments or specific types.
  - Enum: `all`, `preview`, `prod_deployment_urls_and_all_previews`, `all_except_custom_domains`
- **password** (string, nullable) - Optional - The password to protect project deployments. Maximum length: 72 characters.

##### ssoProtection
- **deploymentType** (string) - Required - Specify if SSO protection applies to all deployments or specific types.
  - Default: `preview`
  - Enum: `all`, `preview`, `prod_deployment_urls_and_all_previews`, `all_except_custom_domains`
- Description: Ensures visitors to Preview Deployments are logged into Vercel with minimum Viewer access.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "defaultDeploymentProtection": {
    "passwordProtection": {
      "deploymentType": "preview",
      "password": "secure-password-123"
    },
    "ssoProtection": {
      "deploymentType": "preview"
    }
  }
}
```

### Response Example
```json
{
  "defaultDeploymentProtection": {
    "passwordProtection": {
      "deploymentType": "preview",
      "password": "secure-password-123"
    },
    "ssoProtection": {
      "deploymentType": "preview"
    }
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Deployment Protection Settings

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/reference/endpoints/teams/update-a-team

Configure default deployment protection mechanisms for team deployments. This includes password protection and SSO protection settings that apply to deployments across the team.

```APIDOC
## Deployment Protection Settings

### Description
Manage default deployment protection settings for team deployments

### Properties

#### defaultDeploymentProtection
- **Type**: object
- **Description**: Default deployment protection for this team
- **Properties**:
  - **passwordProtection** (object) - Password protection configuration
    - **deploymentType** (string, required) - Type of deployment to protect
  - **ssoProtection** (object) - SSO protection configuration
    - **deploymentType** (string, required) - Type of deployment to protect

### Response Example
```json
{
  "defaultDeploymentProtection": {
    "passwordProtection": {
      "deploymentType": "preview"
    },
    "ssoProtection": {
      "deploymentType": "production"
    }
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Vercel Protection Bypass in Playwright Tests

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/security/deployment-protection/methods-to-bypass-deployment-protection/protection-bypass-automation

This Playwright configuration example demonstrates how to set `x-vercel-protection-bypass` and `x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie` headers within the `extraHTTPHeaders` property. This allows Playwright tests to bypass Vercel's protection using environment variables for secrets.

```TypeScript
const config: PlaywrightTestConfig = {
  use: {
    extraHTTPHeaders: {
      'x-vercel-protection-bypass': process.env.VERCEL_AUTOMATION_BYPASS_SECRET,
      'x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie': true | 'samesitenone'
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### PATCH /v9/projects/{idOrName}

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/deployment-protection/methods-to-protect-deployments/password-protection

Update an existing project with password protection settings. This endpoint allows you to enable, update, or disable password protection for your deployments by specifying the deployment type and password in the request body.

```APIDOC
## PATCH /v9/projects/{idOrName}

### Description
Update an existing Vercel project configuration, including password protection settings for deployments.

### Method
PATCH

### Endpoint
/v9/projects/{idOrName}

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **idOrName** (string) - Required - The project ID or name to update

### Request Body
- **passwordProtection** (object) - Optional - Password protection configuration object
  - **deploymentType** (string) - Required - Deployment scope for password protection
    - `prod_deployment_urls_and_all_previews` - Standard Protection (production URLs and all previews)
    - `all` - All Deployments (protect all deployments)
    - `preview` - Only Preview Deployments (protect only preview deployments)
  - **password** (string) - Required - The password for deployment protection

### Enable or Update Password Protection
#### Request Example
```json
{
  "passwordProtection": {
    "deploymentType": "prod_deployment_urls_and_all_previews",
    "password": "your-secure-password"
  }
}
```

### Disable Password Protection
#### Request Example
```json
{
  "passwordProtection": null
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **id** (string) - Project ID
- **name** (string) - Project name
- **passwordProtection** (object) - Updated password protection settings
  - **deploymentType** (string) - Configured deployment type
  - **password** (string) - Password (may be redacted in response)

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "id": "prj_abc123",
  "name": "my-project",
  "passwordProtection": {
    "deploymentType": "prod_deployment_urls_and_all_previews",
    "password": "***"
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Project Security and Protection Settings

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/reference/endpoints/projects/update-an-existing-project

Configure security settings including SSO protection, deployment skew protection, and affected projects deployments.

```APIDOC
## Project Security Configuration

### Description
Security and protection settings for Vercel projects including SSO protection, git connectivity, and deployment controls.

### Properties

#### rootDirectory
- **type**: string or null
- **description**: Root directory for the project

#### serverlessFunctionZeroConfigFailover
- **type**: boolean
- **description**: Enable zero-config failover for serverless functions

#### skewProtectionBoundaryAt
- **type**: number
- **description**: Timestamp boundary for skew protection

#### skewProtectionMaxAge
- **type**: number
- **description**: Maximum age in seconds for skew protection

#### skipGitConnectDuringLink
- **type**: boolean
- **description**: Skip git connection during project linking

#### sourceFilesOutsideRootDirectory
- **type**: boolean
- **description**: Allow source files outside the root directory

#### enableAffectedProjectsDeployments
- **type**: boolean
- **description**: Enable automatic deployments for affected projects in monorepos

#### ssoProtection
- **type**: object or null
- **required**: No
- **description**: SSO protection configuration for deployments

#### ssoProtection.deploymentType
- **type**: string
- **required**: Yes (when ssoProtection is defined)
- **enum**: ["preview", "all", "prod_deployment_urls_and_all_previews", "all_except_custom_domains"]
- **description**: Deployment types to protect with SSO

### Configuration Example
```json
{
  "rootDirectory": "./apps/web",
  "serverlessFunctionZeroConfigFailover": true,
  "skewProtectionBoundaryAt": 1640995200,
  "skewProtectionMaxAge": 86400,
  "skipGitConnectDuringLink": false,
  "sourceFilesOutsideRootDirectory": false,
  "enableAffectedProjectsDeployments": true,
  "ssoProtection": {
    "deploymentType": "all"
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Vercel Authentication via API

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/security/deployment-protection/methods-to-protect-deployments/vercel-authentication

This snippet demonstrates how to enable, update, or disable Vercel Authentication for a project using the Vercel API. It shows the JSON payload required for the `ssoProtection` field to set the deployment protection type (e.g., 'prod_deployment_urls_and_all_previews', 'all', 'preview') or to disable it by setting `ssoProtection` to `null`.

```json
{
  "ssoProtection": {
    "deploymentType": "prod_deployment_urls_and_all_previews" | "all" | "preview"
  }
}
```

```json
{
  "ssoProtection": null
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Vercel Protection Bypass Header or Query Parameter

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/security/deployment-protection/methods-to-bypass-deployment-protection/protection-bypass-automation

This snippet illustrates the required `x-vercel-protection-bypass` header or query parameter. It is essential for bypassing Vercel's project protection during automation and must include the project's generated secret.

```HTTP Header
x-vercel-protection-bypass: your-generated-secret (required)
```

--------------------------------

### Enable or Update Trusted IPs using Vercel API

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/security/deployment-protection/methods-to-protect-deployments/trusted-ips

Configure Trusted IPs for Vercel deployments via API endpoint. Specify deployment type (all, preview, production, or prod_deployment_urls_and_all_previews), add IPv4 or CIDR addresses with optional notes, and set protection mode. Use null value to disable Trusted IPs completely.

```json
{
  "trustedIps": {
    "deploymentType": "all" | "preview" | "production" | "prod_deployment_urls_and_all_previews",
    "addresses": [
      {
        "value": "<ipv4_or_cidr>",
        "note": "<optional_description>"
      }
    ],
    "protectionMode": "additional"
  }
}

{
  "trustedIps": null
}
```

--------------------------------

### SSO Protection Configuration

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/reference/endpoints/projects/update-the-data-cache-feature

Manages Single Sign-On protection settings for different deployment types (preview, production). Controls access policies for secured deployments.

```APIDOC
## SSO Protection Configuration Schema

### Description
The SSO Protection Configuration object specifies Single Sign-On protection policies for different deployment environments.

### Configuration Fields

#### Protection Settings
- **deploymentType** (string) - Deployment type to protect (preview, production, all)

### Response Example
```json
{
  "deploymentType": "preview"
}
```
```
